Document [0] (Title: Fingolimod -- Indications -- FDA-Approved Indications) The efficacy of fingolimod in treating RRMS has been demonstrated in 3 separate double-blind, randomized, phase III clinical trials. In the placebo-controlled FREEDOMS trial (FTY720 Research Evaluating Effects of Daily Oral Therapy in Multiple Sclerosis), fingolimod demonstrated improved relapse rate, decreased disease disability progression, reduced number of active or enlarging magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) lesions, and brain atrophy over 2 years. The FREEDOMS II trial evaluated fingolimod's safety profile and found that daily administration of 0.5 mg had a favorable benefit-risk ratio when treating RRMS.

Document [3] (Title: Fingolimod -- Contraindications) Fingolimod is contraindicated in patients who have experienced cardiovascular events within the last 6 months, including myocardial infarction, unstable angina, stroke, transient ischemic attack, class III/IV heart failure, or decompensated heart failure requiring hospitalization. Additionally, patients with a history of Mobitz type II heart block, third-degree AV block, or sick sinus syndrome should avoid fingolimod unless they have a working pacemaker. Fingolimod can potentially prolong the QTc interval; therefore, physicians should refrain from initiating fingolimod treatment in patients with a baseline QTc of 500 ms or longer. [2]
Document [4] (Title: Cerebrovascular Disease -- Treatment / Management) Acute ischemic stroke patients who meet the criteria for rtPA and do not have any contraindications should receive IV rtPA. Patients who have large vessel occlusions should be evaluated for possible endovascular intervention. All patients suspected of having an acute ischemic stroke should be admitted for a full neurological workup.  Neurology consultation should be obtained. The workup of acute ischemic stroke includes a search for a source of thrombus, which includes carotid artery evaluation by ultrasound, CTA, MRA, or conventional angiography.  A transthoracic echocardiogram is obtained to ascertain for low ejection fraction, the cardiac source of the clot, or patent foramen ovale.  EKG and telemetry are obtained to ascertain for rhythms predisposing to stroke such as atrial fibrillation.  Labs such as a fasting lipid panel, and hemoglobin A1C, are obtained to ascertain for modifiable risk factors for stroke.  Other labs such as a hypercoagulable panel in young patients or B12 and syphilis testing in selected patients is also obtained. Antiplatelet and statins remain the mainstay of medical management of stroke.
Document [5] (Title: Chronic Transplantation Rejection(Archived) -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials) FTY720 (fingolimod) is a novel immunosuppressive agent investigated over the past few decades. FTY has proven highly effective in prolonging graft survival in preclinical models of transplantation and multiple sclerosis. Unlike traditional immunosuppressants, FTY720 is an immunomodulator with a unique mechanism of action, classified as a sphingosine 1-phosphate receptor (S1P-R) modulator. [8] [9] In laboratory animals, activating S1P receptors with fingolimod triggers anti-inflammatory and anti-apoptotic pathways, enhancing myocardial salvage by reducing cardiac fibrosis. [10]

Document [7] (Title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 2 -- Community 572) The community describes a network of ischemic heart disease centered on acute coronary insufficiency (إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ), classified under acute ischemic heart disease, unspecified, and localized to the coronary arteries (věnčité tepny). Specific downstream coronary manifestations include coronary artery spasm (تشنج الشريان التاجي), coronary artery disease (مرض الشريان التاجي), chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease (مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد), coronary arterial sclerosis (تصلب عصيدي في التاجي), coronary artery inflammation (التهاب الشريان التاجي), and coronary artery rupture. The left coronary artery is composed of branches of the coronary arteries. Therapeutically, antiplatelet agents tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochloride may prevent progression of إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, whereas fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochloride, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, ketamine and ketamine hydrochloride are contraindicated in patients with إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ or heart failure. Lipid‑lowering and anti‑arteriosclerosis drugs, exemplified by niacinamide, are associated with management of elevated triglycerides (ارتفاع نسبة الدهون الثلاثية في الدم) and may help prevent coronary artery disease. Document [20] (Title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 2 -- Community 538) Acute coronary insufficiency (إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ) is an acute ischemic heart disease localized to the coronary arteries (věnčité tepny) of the left coronary artery system. Pharmacologically, tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochloride are identified as agents that can prevent acute coronary insufficiency, whereas fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochloride, and fingolimod lauryl sulfate are contraindicated in this condition. Document [26] (Title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 1241) Myocardial infarction (إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ) is classified within the MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS category and is a child of vascular ischemia. It serves as a parent condition for coronary disease (مرض التاجي) and chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease (مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد). Myocardial infarction is clinically linked to coronary artery atherosclerosis (تصلب الشريان التاجي) and frequently co‑occurs with palpitations (خفقانات). Coronary artery atherosclerosis is associated with a general cardiac disorder (اضطراب قلبي) and heart murmur (نفخة قلبية); its evaluation commonly employs CT coronary angiography (تصوير الأوعية التاجية بالفحص المقطعي المحوسب). Both myocardial infarction and coronary artery atherosclerosis are related to heart failure (فشل قلبي), and the MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS group has a narrower relationship with heart failure. Chest tightness (خُنَاقٌ صَدْرِيّ) is also linked to heart failure. Pharmacologically, tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochloride may prevent myocardial infarction, whereas fingolimod (including its hydrochloride and lauryl sulfate forms) and ketamine hydrochloride are contraindicated in patients with heart failure; fingolimod is additionally contraindicated in myocardial infarction. The specific cardiac disorder “أم دم الشريان التاجي” is a narrow subtype of a general cardiac disorder and is a child of coronary disease (مرض التاجي), representing a particular form of coronary pathology. CT coronary angiography is directly used to diagnose and monitor coronary disease, including its subtypes such as “أم دم الشريان التاجي” and coronary artery atherosclerosis, underscoring the integrated nature of ischemic cardiac conditions, their clinical manifestations, diagnostic imaging, and therapeutic considerations.
Document [27] (Title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2302) Migraine with ischemic complication is a broader clinical category that encompasses forms of migraine in which vascular ischemia occurs, and persistent migraine aura with cerebral infarction is a specific manifestation within this category. Persistent migraine aura with cerebral infarction is also classified as an acute cerebrovascular disease, reflecting its nature as a sudden, stroke‑like event caused by interruption of cerebral blood flow during a prolonged migraine aura. By linking migraine with ischemic complication to acute cerebrovascular disease through the specific entity of persistent migraine aura with cerebral infarction, the knowledge underscores that certain prolonged migraine auras can evolve into acute cerebral infarctions, highlighting the importance of recognizing these migraine‑related ischemic complications for timely diagnosis and appropriate stroke‑focused management.

This procedure falls under the broader category استئصال باطنة الشريان (arterial endarterectomy), which is indicated for cases encompassed by حالات الخثار والانصمام الشريانية (SMQ).
